approach: HITL doctrine change to SDD fixed-universal bars (loaded by every SDD/ACED project) — emit verdict and stop at both gates; no self-ratify

One principle, one duty per actor per gate (the builder-spec/builder-impl split shape): suite-format states the Given's status + the spec-side authoring duty; impl-producer states the deliver duty; impl-judge detects
Both copying directions closed: producer-absorbs-suite (the #210 mechanism) and spec-absorbs-impl (live on every revise/backfill)
Additive scenarios only on two frozen .feature files — self-clear, no re-open, no Clearance
Trap pinned in the impl-judge polarity: a match is the finding, a mismatch is health, unclassifiable escalates
Prevalence sweep (issue item 3) deferred to a follow-up CR — retroactive semantic corpus audit needs its own node

why: Issue #220 root-cause follow-up from #211: nothing stopped the impl-producer absorbing the frozen suite's Givens, and the impl gate is blind to it by construction (an absorbed scenario passes maximally). Missing rule now specified: a Given is a test vector, not specification. HITL doctrine change to SDD fixed-universal bars, ratified in-session by the Council; leash auto-none, no self-assert. FOUR nodes, one principle with a distinct duty per actor per gate (the builder-spec/builder-impl split shape, judged non-duplicative): authoring/suite-format (reference, canonical rule) + authoring/spec-producer (+4 scenarios) + mission/impl-producer (+5) + mission/impl-judge (+11). TWO refinements beyond the issue, both judge-hardened: (1) precondition-vs-apparatus split — the issue's one-liner 'owes nothing to the Given' is a compression that read literally would make producers drop real preconditions and break the contract, so a Given fixes a precondition (contract) and apparatus (test vector, binds nothing); (2) the swap test as the operational discriminator — substitute the Given's domain, and if the Then still holds it was apparatus, so a producer's own label decides nothing. Both copying directions closed at the gate where each is detectable: impl-absorbs-suite (the git-verified #210 mechanism) and spec-absorbs-impl (live on every revise/backfill, the mechanism #211's filer wrongly assumed had fired). Trap preserved: a mismatch between suite probes and artifact examples is deliberate independence, NEVER drift — stated as a polarity table, prose, and 3 dedicated scenarios so no reconcile loop re-contaminates ssa-lowering. Prevalence sweep (issue item 3) deferred to a follow-up CR; the lexical-probe-is-unfit finding is preserved durably in suite-format ('judged, not linted'). Three cold sdd-spec-judge rounds, each a fresh context: R1 architect FAIL (new section header split an existing section, stranding 4 rollup scenarios) -> fixed by pure relocation; R2 oracle+builder FAIL, refuting a CONDUCTOR decision not a producer slip (the 'no spec-producer.feature change' call was an unverified coverage claim — the producer's only self-check is the MECHANICAL form check, which this diff's own text declares is not the right check for a semantic bar, leaving the reverse-direction duty as prose with zero observable scenarios while impl-producer got 5) -> fixed by covering, not by narrowing the ratified scope; R3 ALIGNED true, oracle/builder/architect all PASS, 0 failing, no blocker. R3's own architect observation (the 'label decides nothing' closure had no scenario) closed with 1 additive scenario and confirmed by R3. Edit class classified structurally per named Scenario via gherkin-cli diff, never a raw line diff: spec-producer added:4, impl-producer added:5, impl-judge added:11, all modified:0 removed:0 addOnly:true -> additive, self-clear, all three stay @frozen, no re-open, no Clearance floor. Mechanical pre-filter green before every judge round (check:specs exit=0).
